text: I'll ingest this into your wiki. Let me start by reading the config and scanning existing wiki pages.

content:
1	# Second Brain Wiki Configuration
2	
3	This file configures the skill for Evan's environment. Edit paths and
4	projects as your work evolves.
5	
6	---
7	
8	## Paths
9	
10	| Path | Value | Description |
11	|------|-------|-------------|
12	| Wiki directory | `~/second-brain/wiki/` | Where wiki pages live |
13	| Sources directory | `~/second-brain/sources/` | Raw source files stored here |
14	| Inbox directory | `~/Desktop/Second Brain Inbox/` | Drop files here for processing |
15	
16	---
17	
18	## Active Projects
19	
20	These are your current projects and areas of focus. The skill uses these
21	to find connections when ingesting new content.
22	
23	- **evaluator-super-agent** — automated prototype quality assessment using persona-driven walkthroughs, 7-dimension rubric scoring, actor/evaluator separation
24	- **prototype-creator** — generates clickable prototypes from RFEs, tiered AC classification, Playwright automation
25	- **agent-eval-harness** — framework for evaluating AI skill quality, eval.yaml configs, judges, scoring pipelines
26	- **ai-sdlc-pipeline** — three-phase pipeline: RFE Creator (WHAT) → Prototype Creator (SHOW) → Strat Creator (HOW)
27	- **my-cursor-claw** — personal workspace with 20+ skills, MCP connections, notetaking, automations
28	- **decision-kit** — Decision Driven Development toolkit, /strategize, /decide, /journal
29	- **automated-usability-testing** — simulated usability testing tool, persona YAML files, evaluate-flow protocol
30	- **archie** — UX research agent for the RHOAIUX team
31	- **rhoai-prototypes** — RHOAI prototype collection for evaluation
32	
33	---
34	
35	## Calendar Integration
36	
37	| Setting | Value |
38	|---------|-------|
39	| Calendar name | `primary` |
40	| Auto-ingest meetings | `true` |
41	| Minimum meeting duration | `15 min` |
42	| Require notes/transcript | `true` |
43	| Skip recurring without notes | `true` |
44	
45	Events that qualify for auto-ingestion:
46	- Have attached meeting notes or a description with substance (not just "Weekly sync")
47	- Have a linked Google Doc (agenda, transcript, action items)
48	- Duration is 15+ minutes
49	- Are not recurring meetings that had no notes added
50	
51	---
52	
53	## MCP Integrations
54	
55	| Integration | MCP Server | Used for |
56	|-------------|-----------|----------|
57	| Google Docs | `user-google-workspace` | Extracting doc content |
58	| Google Calendar | `user-google-workspace` | Meeting ingestion |
59	| Slack | `project-0-my-cursor-claw-slack` | Channel message ingestion |
60	
61	---
62	
63	## Cross-Link Priority
64	
65	When multiple connections exist, prioritize these categories (from the
66	wiki index):
67	
68	1. **Red Hat AI-SDLC** — pipeline components, RFE/prototype/strat flow
69	2. **Evaluation & Quality** — rubrics, heuristics, eval harness
70	3. **Tools & Skills** — decision kit, cursor claw, skill overlays
71	4. **Meetings** — meeting notes with action items and decisions
72	5. **Standups** — sprint progress and blockers
73	6. **Session Logs** — what was built and shipped
74

1	# Wiki Page Template
2	
3	Use this template when creating new wiki pages. Copy the structure,
4	fill in the bracketed fields.
5	
6	---
7	
8	## Template
9	
10	```markdown
11	# <Page Title>
12	
13	<One-sentence summary of what this page covers.>
14	
15	## Key Points
16	
17	- <Bullet point 1: a concrete fact, concept, or takeaway>
18	- <Bullet point 2>
19	- <Bullet point 3>
20	- <Additional bullets as needed, max 10>
21	
22	## Related
23	
24	- [<Related Page Title>](<related-slug>.md) — <one-line reason for connection>
25	- [<Another Related Page>](<slug>.md) — <connection reason>
26	
27	## Sources
28	
29	- <path/to/source/file or URL where this came from>
30	- Ingested: <YYYY-MM-DD>
31	```
32	
33	---
34	
35	## Good Example
36	
37	```markdown
38	# Retrieval Augmented Generation
39	
40	Technique that grounds LLM responses in retrieved documents rather than
41	relying solely on training data.
42	
43	## Key Points
44	
45	- Combines a retriever (vector search) with a generator (LLM)
46	- Reduces hallucination by providing source documents as context
47	- Trade-off: retrieval quality limits generation quality
48	- Common stack: embeddings → vector DB → reranker → LLM prompt
49	- Works best when source corpus is well-chunked and up-to-date
50	
51	## Related
52	
53	- [Vector Databases](vector-databases.md) — the retrieval layer in RAG pipelines
54	- [Agent Eval Harness](agent-eval-harness.md) — uses embedding similarity for judge calibration
55	
56	## Sources
57	
58	- ~/second-brain/sources/what-is-rag.md
59	- Ingested: 2026-06-10
60	```
61	
62	---
63	
64	## Bad Example
65	
66	```markdown
67	# RAG
68	
69	Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) is a technique in artificial intelligence
70	that combines information retrieval with text generation. It was first proposed
71	by Lewis et al. in 2020. The basic idea is that instead of relying solely on
72	what a language model learned during training, you can augment it with relevant
73	documents retrieved at inference time.
74	
75	This approach has several advantages. First, it reduces hallucination because
76	the model can reference actual documents. Second, it allows the model to access
77	up-to-date information that wasn't in its training data. Third, it provides
78	transparency because you can trace responses back to source documents.
79	
80	There are several implementations of RAG available today...
81	```
82	
83	**Why it's bad:**
84	- Reads like a Wikipedia article, not a wiki node
85	- No bullet summaries (walls of prose)
86	- No Related section (isolated page, no connections)
87	- No Sources section (where did this come from?)
88	- Title is an acronym without expansion
89	- Contains filler ("There are several..." "This approach has...")
90

=== agent-eval-harness.md ===
# Agent Eval Harness

A framework for evaluating AI agent skill quality through automated testing, judge scoring, and iterative optimization.

=== ai-first-sync-2026-06-25.md ===

Strategy session on integrating the UXD AI Helpers repo with Ansible's Carbonite harness. First major external consumer of the centralized skill distribution model.

=== cursor-work-2026-06-16.md ===
# Cursor Agent Work — June 16, 2026

A daily record of all agent work done across 4 Cursor sessions on this date. Focused on productivity infrastructure: skills that make other skills better, zero-friction workflows, and manager-oriented accountability.

=== decision-kit.md ===
# Decision Kit

A toolkit for Decision Driven Development. AI does the busywork around your decisions — you make the calls.

=== evaluator-super-agent.md ===
# Evaluator Super Agent

Evan's primary assignment: build an evaluation agent that automates prototype quality assessment within the [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) pipeline.

=== my-cursor-claw.md ===

Personal skill development workspace built during the Day of Learning Agent Skills Workshop (2026-06-05). Contains 20+ custom Cursor skills, 5 MCP server connections, and automated daily review infrastructure.

=== nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md ===

# Outcome Tiger Team

Proposal by Andy Braren to form a cross-functional tiger team focused on the critical step *before* the [AI-SDLC Pipeline](ai-sdlc-pipeline.md): creating, prioritizing, and visualizing **outcomes** — the directional goals that determine which features get prioritized and why.

=== prototype-creator.md ===
# Prototype Creator

Phase 2 ("the SHOW") of Red Hat's [AI-SDLC Pipeline](ai-sdlc-pipeline.md). Takes RFE user stories and produces rapid prototypes that make the proposed experience tangible before engineering commits.

=== redhat-ai-first-lessons.md ===
# Red Hat AI-First Lessons

Jeremy Eder's (Distinguished Engineer, 16 years at Red Hat) presentation on lessons from Red Hat Engineering on AI-first software development. 38-slide deck covering the agentic SDLC, issue-to-PR bots, evaluation infrastructure, and organizational change.

=== retrieval-augmented-generation.md ===
# Retrieval-Augmented Generation

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is a technique that enhances LLM responses by retrieving relevant documents from an external knowledge base before generating an answer. Instead of relying solely on the model's training data, RAG grounds responses in real, up-to-date documents.

Phase 1 ("the WHAT") of Red Hat's [AI-SDLC Pipeline](ai-sdlc-pipeline.md). Claude Code skills for creating, reviewing, and submitting RFEs (Requests for Enhancement) to the RHAIRFE Jira project.

=== vector-databases.md ===
# Vector Databases

A vector database stores data as high-dimensional vectors (embeddings) and enables fast similarity search. They are a key component in [RAG](retrieval-augmented-generation.md) pipelines, recommendation systems, and semantic search.

=== vector-similarity-search.md ===
# Vector Similarity Search

Core technique for finding semantically similar content using vector embeddings — the engine behind modern search, recommendations, and deduplication.

1	# Agent Eval Harness
2	
3	A framework for evaluating AI agent skill quality through automated testing, judge scoring, and iterative optimization.
4	
5	## How It Works
6	
7	1. **eval.yaml** — defines execution mode, dataset schema, output descriptions, judges, models, and thresholds
8	2. **Dataset** — test cases with realistic inputs for the skill
9	3. **Judges** — automated scorers that rate skill outputs against criteria
10	4. **Run** — executes the skill against test cases, scores with judges, reports results
11	5. **Optimize** — identifies failures, edits SKILL.md, re-runs to verify, checks for regressions
12	
13	## Key Skills (from the harness)
14	
15	- `/eval-analyze` — examine a skill and generate eval.yaml
16	- `/eval-dataset` — generate test cases for a skill
17	- `/eval-run` — execute evaluation against test cases
18	- `/eval-optimize` — automated improvement loop
19	- `/eval-review` — interactive human review of results
20	
21	## The Optimize Loop
22	
23	```
24	Run eval → Identify judge failures → Read traces → Edit SKILL.md → Re-run → Verify
25	```
26	
27	This is directly applicable to the [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) work — the same pattern of rubric-based scoring, automated iteration, and quality gates.
28	
29	## MLflow Integration
30	
31	Results can be logged to MLflow for tracking, dataset syncing, and trace-level feedback.
32	
33	## Related
34	
35	- [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) — uses rubric-based scoring for prototypes
36	- [UX Quality Rubric](ux-quality-rubric.md) — the 4-dimension scoring system
37	- [My Cursor Claw](my-cursor-claw.md) — includes run-evals skill
38	- [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) — applies eval patterns to prototype evaluation
39	- [Red Hat AI-First Lessons](redhat-ai-first-lessons.md) — Jeremy Eder's presentation referencing EvalHub, Harbor, MLflow, and the harness
40	- [Ansible AI Helpers Strategy — 2026-06-17](ansible-ai-helpers-strategy-2026-06-17.md) — eval patterns relevant to measuring heuristic eval pilot results
41	- [Skill Overlays](skill-overlays.md) — overlays improve agent accuracy, directly relevant to eval quality
42	- [WG: UXD Efficiency — June 29](wg-uxd-efficiency-2026-06-29.md) — token efficiency and cost management for eval runs
43	
44	## Sources
45	
46	- sources/2026-06-04-agent-eval-harness-overview.html
47	- opendatahub-io agent-eval-harness skills (installed as plugins)
48

content:
1	# Skill Overlays
2	
3	LLM-generated, project-specific context files that sit on top of general-purpose skills. Instead of re-inferring a project's structure on every run, you pre-generate a dense context layer capturing architecture, naming conventions, patterns, and implicit standards.
4	
5	## Key Concepts
6	
7	- An LLM analyzes project metadata (source code, git history, schemas, config files)
8	- Generates context files capturing the project's implicit guidelines and conventions
9	- Overlay files sit alongside general-purpose skills and provide project-specific grounding
10	- The agent loads the overlay as part of its context when working on that project
11	- Pattern is language/framework/domain agnostic
12	
13	## Concrete Example
14	
15	In the data products ADLC (Carl Trieloff's org), an MCP server connects to Snowflake and reads metadata for all data products. An LLM generates skill files (one per data product) with specific context about structure, lineage, and naming conventions. Before overlays, the agent was "assuming certain things" and making inference errors. After adding overlays, implementation quality "went drastically cleaner."
16	
17	## Open Questions
18	
19	- **Dynamic vs. static:** Runtime generation via MCP avoids staleness but adds latency/cost. Checked-in files are fast but drift.
20	- **Scale:** How to generate and maintain overlays across hundreds of repos?
21	- **Freshness:** How to detect when an overlay is stale enough to regenerate?
22	
23	## Who Validated It
24	
25	- Carl Trieloff / Nathan Chandirasegarane's team (data products ADLC). Concept invented Apr 4, 2026, first tested Apr 7.
26	- UIE adopting the pattern for harness engineering work.
27	
28	## Related
29	
30	- [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) -- overlays are relevant to the team-specific overlay approach for prototype evaluation
31	- [Agent Eval Harness](agent-eval-harness.md) -- overlays improve agent accuracy, directly relevant to eval quality
32	- [My Cursor Claw](my-cursor-claw.md) -- the claw already uses project-specific context (skills, rules), overlays formalize this pattern
33	- [AI-SDLC Pipeline](ai-sdlc-pipeline.md) -- overlays plug into the pipeline as a context engineering technique
34	
35	## Sources
36	
37	- ~/Desktop/Second Brain Inbox/skill-overlays.md
38

1	# Evaluator Super Agent
2	
3	Evan's primary assignment: build an evaluation agent that automates prototype quality assessment within the [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) pipeline.
4	
5	**Status: Shipped.** The evaluator is a 3-phase skill pipeline on branch `evan/prototype-evaluate` at [andybraren/prototype-creator](https://github.com/andybraren/prototype-creator). It has evaluated 18+ STRATs against Jira acceptance criteria with Playwright persona walkthroughs.
6	
7	## What It Does (current)
8	
9	| Capability | Source | Status |
10	|---|---|---|
11	| AC evaluation | Custom Playwright persona journeys against Jira criteria | Shipped |
12	| Usability scoring | 7-dimension rubric with persona overlays (Zack Bodnar's framework) | Shipped |
13	| Consistency checking | Beau Morley's consistency checker (source + visual mode) | Shipped |
14	| Navigation reachability | Playwright verifies pages are clickable from sidebar | Shipped |
15	| Human review | Review tab in HTML report with override buttons + CSV writeback | Shipped |
16	| Report hosting | GitLab Pages (eval-reports-7ebc0e.pages.redhat.com) | Shipped |
17	| Google Sheet sync | Automated pass/fail + usability metrics to tracking sheet | Shipped |
18	| Feedback loop | Eval results → prototype-refine → re-eval (max 3 iterations) | In progress |
19	
20	## Architecture (confirmed June 16 with Andy)
21	
22	Evolved from "single monolithic super agent" to a **phased pipeline with overlays**:
23	
24	```
25	Phase 1: prototype-evaluate (extract ACs, RFE, Outcome, personas, journeys)
26	    ↓ writes extract-state.json
27	Phase 2: evaluate-journey (Playwright walkthroughs, consistency check)
28	    ↓ writes journey-log.json, screenshots, CSV
29	Phase 3: evaluate-usability (7-dimension persona scoring)
30	    ↓ updates journey-log.json
31	Phase 4: render-report.js + sync-sheet.js (HTML, CSV, Google Sheet)
32	```
33	
34	**Key design decisions:**
35	1. **Outcomes over Strategic Goals** as grounding criteria (Outcomes are consistently written)
36	2. **One Playwright pass** — shared screenshots across usability, consistency, and AC checks
37	3. **98% generic + 2% product overlay** (`config/product-overlay.yaml`) — portable to other products
38	4. **Hint mechanism** for dead-end navigation (failure logged but journey continues)
39	5. **Human-in-the-loop** — Review tab for designer input before feedback loop acts
40	
41	## Research References
42	
43	- **Avenir-UX** — autonomous UX evaluation agent using SEQ/SUS + persona framework
44	- **UXCascade** — multi-agent persona simulation with severity-rated issues (S1–S4)
45	- GitLab Notes REST API for MR comment posting
46	- Danger bot framework for CI-integrated review automation
47	
48	## How It Connects
49	
50	```
51	Jira STRAT (Outcome → RFE → STRAT)
52	    → prototype-create (generates code in workspace)
53	    → prototype-evaluate (this agent — 3 phases)
54	        ├── Phase 1: Extract ACs, personas, journeys from Jira
55	        ├── Phase 2: Playwright journeys + consistency check
56	        ├── Phase 3: Usability dimension scoring
57	        └── Phase 4: Report + Sheet sync
58	    → Human reviews report (override verdicts, add notes)
59	    → prototype-refine (acts on suggestions + human annotations)
60	    → Re-evaluate (loop until PASS or max iterations)
61	    → prototype-submit (MR to GitLab)
62	```
63	
64	## Key Design Inputs from Demo (May 14)
65	
66	From the [Prototype Creator Demo](prototype-creator-demo-may14.md):
67	- Consistency checkers should only evaluate what's NEW (CSS class="new" tagging)
68	- Even auto-mode should produce decision-kit pages for post-review
69	- Evaluator needs outcome-level awareness, not just feature-level
70	- Preview URL is the evaluation entry point (load the MR URL, assess in browser context)
71	- Token efficiency matters — scope evaluation to changed areas only
72	
73	## Related
74	
75	- [Outcome Tiger Team](outcome-tiger-team.md) — proposes outcome-level awareness for evaluation, directly extending this agent's scope
76	- [Prototype Creator Demo — May 14](prototype-creator-demo-may14.md) — walkthrough with design insights for this agent
77	- [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) — the pipeline this plugs into
78	- [UX Quality Rubric](ux-quality-rubric.md) — existing scoring to extend
79	- [Agent Eval Harness](agent-eval-harness.md) — pattern for automated eval loops
80	- [Decision Kit](decision-kit.md) — using /strategize to plan this agent
81	- [Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge](nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md) — informs methodology choice
82	- [My Cursor Claw](my-cursor-claw.md) — the workspace where this will be built
83	- [Ansible AI Helpers Strategy — 2026-06-17](ansible-ai-helpers-strategy-2026-06-17.md) — heuristic eval pilot connects to automated evaluation vision
84	
85	## Updates from June 16, 2026 Review
86	
87	Decisions made with Andy Braren (see [Andy/Evan Eval Review — June 16](andy-evan-eval-review-2026-06-16.md)):
88	
89	- **Architecture confirmed:** Centralized super evaluator, not fragmented skills. One Playwright pass, shared screenshots.
90	- **Grounding confirmed:** Outcomes as primary criteria, Strategic Goals excluded for now.
91	- **Overlay approach adopted:** 98% generic + 2% product-specific layer. See [GitLab skill-overlays best practice](https://gitlab.cee.redhat.com/global-engineering/wg-agentic-sdlc/-/blob/main/best-practices/skill-overlays.md).
92	- **Hint mechanism shipped:** Simulated UX researcher provides next URL on failure, allowing evaluation to continue while logging failure impact.
93	- **Screenshot consolidation:** Sub-agents share one set of Playwright screenshots instead of each capturing independently.
94	
95	- [RH AI-First UX — 2026-06-18](rh-ai-first-ux-2026-06-18.md) — Evan demoed automated usability testing + PatternFly consistency checker to full team
96	- [Andy/Evan Meeting — June 22](andy-evan-2026-06-22.md) — eval iteration history, flagging system, Prototype Creator integration strategy
97	- [AI-First Sync — June 25](ai-first-sync-2026-06-25.md) — Evan's full evaluator presentation to the team, readiness check discussion
98	- [RH AI-First UX — June 25](rh-ai-first-ux-2026-06-25.md) — testing fragmentation concerns, unified testing proposal
99	- [Standup — June 29](standup-2026-06-29.md) — 2 automated usability testing agents in development
100	- [Skill Overlays](skill-overlays.md) — context engineering technique relevant to team-specific overlays
101	- [Andy/Evan Meeting — June 29](andy-evan-2026-06-29.md) — findings tab, auto-fix vs report-only modes, X-ray vs blind evaluation, centralized dashboard
102	- [Midpoint Check-in — June 30](midpoint-checkin-2026-06-30.md) — Zack: "pioneering work" combining research + AI + SDLC; report must be immediately actionable for designers
103	- [Weekly Design Sync — July 1](weekly-design-sync-2026-07-01.md) — Evan to demo evaluator next week; Nielsen's heuristics confirmed as base rubric

input.content:
# Prompt Engineering Best Practices

Core techniques for writing effective LLM prompts that produce consistent, high-quality outputs.

## Key Points

- **Specify output format explicitly** — tell the model what structure you expect (JSON, bullets, table, prose) rather than inferring it
- **Provide examples of good and bad output** — few-shot examples anchor the model more reliably than descriptions alone
- **Use chain-of-thought for complex reasoning** — instruct the model to reason step-by-step before answering; improves accuracy on multi-hop problems
- **Break multi-step tasks into sequential prompts** — a pipeline of focused prompts outperforms one sprawling mega-prompt
- **Include constraints and boundaries explicitly** — state what the model should NOT do as clearly as what it should

## Related

- [Agent Eval Harness](agent-eval-harness.md) — judge prompts are the core of the eval harness; format specs and CoT directly improve scoring accuracy
- [Skill Overlays](skill-overlays.md) — overlays are structured prompts with explicit constraints and project-specific examples baked in
- [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) — each evaluation phase uses format-controlled output and sequential prompting across phases

## Sources

- ~/second-brain/sources/2026-06-15-prompt-engineering-best-practices.md
- Ingested: 2026-07-09
